items.definition_en: A crosswind is wind that blows across your direction of travel.
items.example_en: The plane struggled to land because of a strong crosswind.

items.definition_en: A downburst is a strong downward rush of wind from a storm.
items.example_en: The trees fell over after a downburst hit the area.

items.definition_en: A microburst is a very localized, intense downburst of wind.
items.example_en: Pilots are trained to recognize the dangers of a microburst.

items.definition_en: A trade wind is a steady wind that blows towards the equator.
items.example_en: Sailors relied on the trade wind to cross the ocean.

items.definition_en: A sea breeze is a cool wind that blows from the sea to the land.
items.example_en: A refreshing sea breeze cooled the beach in the afternoon.

items.definition_en: A land breeze is a wind that blows from the land to the sea at night.
items.example_en: Fishermen set sail early helped by the land breeze.

items.definition_en: A jet stream is a strong, fast wind high in the atmosphere.
items.example_en: Planes sometimes fly faster when they enter a jet stream.

items.definition_en: An updraft is a rising current of air.
items.example_en: Birds use an updraft to soar higher in the sky.

items.definition_en: A downdraft is a downward current of air.
items.example_en: The downdraft pushed the smoke quickly to the ground.

items.definition_en: Wind shear is a sudden change in wind speed or direction.
items.example_en: Wind shear can create dangerous flying conditions for pilots.

items.definition_en: A foehn is a warm, dry wind that blows down mountains.
items.example_en: A foehn melted the snow in the valley quickly.

items.definition_en: A sirocco is a hot, dry wind from North Africa.
items.example_en: The city was covered in dust after a sirocco.

items.definition_en: A monsoon wind is a seasonal wind that brings heavy rain.
items.example_en: Farmers depend on the monsoon wind for water.

items.definition_en: A katabatic wind is a cold wind that flows down slopes or mountains.
items.example_en: The katabatic wind made the valley much colder at night.

items.definition_en: An anabatic wind is a wind that moves upward along a hill or mountain.
items.example_en: Gliders waited for the afternoon anabatic wind to take off.

items.definition_en: A squall line is a row of thunderstorms with strong winds.
items.example_en: A squall line moved in and caused heavy wind damage.

items.definition_en: A wind surge is a sudden increase in wind strength.
items.example_en: The wind surge toppled the old fence in minutes.

items.definition_en: A wind tunnel is a structure used to test how wind moves over objects.
items.example_en: The car was tested in a wind tunnel for better aerodynamics.

items.definition_en: A wind farm is an area with many wind turbines to produce electricity.
items.example_en: Wind farms are common in windy regions.

items.definition_en: A zephyr is a gentle, mild wind.
items.example_en: A soft zephyr cooled us during our picnic.
